capture_hook.py analysis: 20 functions, 0 tests. Key untested critical paths: (1) _contains_credentials() — the security firewall for memory writes; (2) find_duplicates() — DEDUP_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD=0.85 logic; (3) handle_user_prompt_submit() — real-time capture on every message; (4) handle_stop() — session-end memory persistence to FTS5 store; (5) _extract_memories_from_message() — LLM-style extraction from assistant text. Suggesting a test task if desktop is queuing work: test_capture_hook.py targeting these 5 critical paths. File is 664 LOC, zero tests = highest risk module in the entire system.

FIX: cca-wrap-worker.md Step 6 terminal close updated. Old: 'close (first window whose frontmost is true)' — fails when Claude Code built-in shell is active (no frontmost Terminal window). New: [[ $TERM_PROGRAM == 'Apple_Terminal' ]] && osascript -e 'tell application Terminal to close front window'. Guard skips silently in Claude Code's shell, works correctly when worker runs in Terminal.app tab via launch_worker.sh. Committed. Apply same fix to cca-auto-worker.md if it has a close step.

OBSERVATION: cca-wrap-worker.md Step 6 terminal close was using 'close (first window whose frontmost is true)' which fails when Claude Code's built-in shell is active — Terminal.app has no frontmost window, returns Invalid index (-1719). Fixed to: [[ $TERM_PROGRAM == 'Apple_Terminal' ]] && osascript -e 'tell application Terminal to close front window'. Guard skips silently in Claude Code shell, works in Terminal.app tab. Committed 437e3ce. | ADVANCEMENT TIP: $TERM_PROGRAM is the reliable env var for detecting terminal host (Apple_Terminal / iTerm.app / vscode / unset in Claude Code shell) — use it as a guard before any osascript Terminal.app calls to prevent errors when running in Claude Code's built-in shell.

Advancement tip: $TERM_PROGRAM guards osascript Terminal.app calls — use it before any close/activate commands to avoid 'Invalid index' errors in Claude Code's built-in shell. Standing directive active from this session: all future advancement tips will be commed to desktop automatically.

Advancement tip: When testing safety guards with regex pattern lists, write one test per pattern (not one test for 'all blocked'). This gives 1:1 traceability — a test failure tells you exactly which pattern broke, not just 'something is wrong with validation'.

Advancement tip: Threshold boundary tests (exactly-at, one-below, one-above) catch regressions that normal tests miss — if KILL_PNL_THRESHOLD is accidentally changed from -30 to -300, only a boundary test at exactly -30.0 will detect it.

Advancement tip: When writing extended test suites for modules with store/dedup logic (like ImprovementStore), use fixture data with unique 'file' fields in pattern_data — the dedup key is (pattern_type, file), so same-type proposals without a file key will collide and be filtered. This catches real behavioral edge cases and validates the dedup boundary correctly.

Fix paper digest spam in cross_chat_queue.jsonl — 'Bayesian Trading (score 85)' sent ~15 times. Add debounce/dedup logic to the hook that writes to cross_chat_queue.jsonl. Before appending a new entry, check if an identical or near-identical message was already sent within the last 24 hours. If so, skip. Write tests for the debounce logic. TDD.

Write tests for any untested CCA module with >200 LOC and 0 tests. Run: find . -name '*.py' -not -name 'test_*' -not -path './.claude/*' | while read f; do lines=$(wc -l < "$f"); test_f="$(dirname $f)/test_$(basename $f)"; if [ $lines -gt 200 ] && [ \! -f "$test_f" ]; then echo "$f ($lines LOC, no tests)"; fi; done — Pick the largest untested file and write comprehensive tests. TDD.

Advancement tip: Hook modules with main() entry points (PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks) consistently had zero integration tests — even when their individual functions were well-covered. Adding main() stdin/stdout tests catches real gaps: env var disabled state, invalid JSON input, empty stdin, and the full decision path. Worth auditing any new hook module for main() coverage on creation.
